header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Poços de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Asking for Directions -- Imperatives & Prepositions -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 4 -- Street Smart -- Asking for Directions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 4 -- Street Smart

listenings.text: Hi Tuca, it's Diego. Welcome to the town! Let me give you directions to the place where we will meet. When you leave the bus station, turn right and walk straight ahead for two blocks. You will pass a small park on your left. At the second traffic light, turn left and cross at the crosswalk. Stay on the sidewalk and keep going until you reach the main square. The coffee shop is on the corner, right next to the old bakery and across from the church. The church is a famous landmark, so it is easy to find. If you get lost, just call me and I will come and meet you. See you soon!

Hi everyone, this is Tuca. Let me tell you how to reach my coffee farm. From the town center, drive straight ahead on the main road for about ten kilometers. When you see the old stone bridge, our most famous landmark, turn left. Do not take the roundabout; go past it and stay on the road. After two more kilometers, you will reach a small intersection next to a blue gate. Turn right there and follow the dirt road until the end. My farmhouse is on the corner, right behind the tall coffee trees. If you get lost, stop at the little church and call me. I cannot wait to welcome you!

extra_audio.text: Tuca leaves the bus station and feels lost. She asks a friendly local named Diego for directions. Diego tells her to go straight ahead and turn right at the intersection. Tuca walks two blocks and reaches the roundabout. She finds the coffee museum on the corner, across from the church.
